steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage ends by stating that understanding how to prevent horizontal gene transfer 'might result in the mitigation of dangerous pathogens.' The question asks what the passage most strongly suggests — so the correct choice must reflect this emphasis on potential real-world application.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A common perception of horizontal gene transfer is inaccurate.: introduces a debate about whether horizontal gene transfer is beneficial or harmful — a framing the passage never presents. ✗
  • Choice B — Horizontal gene transfer is more common than was initially thought.: claims horizontal gene transfer is 'more common' than some alternative — no such comparison appears in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — The results of new research into horizontal gene transfer cast doubt on earlier findings.: suggests a hypothetical controversy about research priorities the passage doesn't address. ✗
  • Choice D — The study of horizontal gene transfer may yield findings with useful applications.: directly reflects the passage's conclusion about mitigating dangerous pathogens. ✓
That matches Choice D — The study of horizontal gene transfer may yield findings with useful applications..
